crime.value: Theft of government submarine plans (Bruce-Partington plans) from a secure office safe, with murder of the thief
crime.quote: The chief dead, Cadogan West dead, our papers stolen.

victim.value: Cadogan West, government clerk with access to secret submarine plans, suspected of treason but actually murdered
victim.quote: That West, of all men, should have done such a thing!

closed_world.value: Government office building requiring three keys for access, limited to few people with authorized entry
closed_world.quote: he would need three keys, would he not, before he could reach the papers?

culprit_and_motive.value: Colonel Walter and foreign agent Oberstein - Walter for money, selling state secrets to European naval powers
culprit_and_motive.quote: Oberstein, eager to complete the coup of his lifetime, came to the lure

resolution.value: Holmes sets a trap with fake advertisement, catches Oberstein, recovers the stolen plans from his trunk
resolution.quote: In his trunk were found the invaluable Bruce-Partington plans

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes, consulting detective called in to solve the government security breach and murder
investigator.quote: It is fortunate for this community that I am not a criminal

suspect_architecture.value: Three main suspects with differentiated access: Cadogan West (trusted clerk with apparent motive), Colonel Walter (key holder with gambling debts), and Oberstein (foreign agent with means to orchestrate). Each has distinct relationship to the stolen plans.
suspect_architecture.quote: West, of all men, should have done such a thing!' 'You are sure of his guilt, then?' 'I can see no other way out of it.'

red_herring.value: Cadogan West framed as the obvious inside traitor - his body found with some plans, his apparent access to the safe, and his suspicious behavior with his fiancée all designed to misdirect from the true perpetrator.
red_herring.quote: That West, of all men, should have done such a thing!

clue_fairness.value: Key distribution explicitly detailed to reader - who has which keys, building security procedures, and the practical impossibility of West's supposed method are all laid out before Holmes's deduction.
clue_fairness.quote: he would need three keys, would he not, before he could reach the papers?

social_world.value: Government office hierarchy and naval security apparatus - the power structure of civil servants, senior clerks, and department heads that both enables and conceals the espionage operation.
social_world.quote: we were as efficient an office as any in the government service

alibi.value: The office closing procedures and key custody create alibis - Johnson as last man out, the watchman's rounds, and the fog providing cover all serve to establish and break alibis.
alibi.quote: I am always the last man out.

reveal_mechanic.value: The trap letter to Oberstein demonstrates the solution - using fake correspondence about missing plans to lure the true perpetrator into revealing himself through his greed to complete the sale.
reveal_mechanic.quote: one essential detail is missing. I have a tracing which will make it complete.

media_and_audience.value: The case exists in 'secret history of a nation' - the public will never know the true scope of the espionage, while institutional face-saving shapes the official narrative.
media_and_audience.quote: secret history of a nation which is often so much more intimate and interesting than its public chronicles

investigator_wound.value: Holmes's enforced inactivity due to the fog - his 'impatient and active nature could endure this drab existence no longer' - makes him hungry for a case worthy of his abilities.
investigator_wound.quote: my comrade's impatient and active nature could endure this drab existence no longer

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the adventure of bruce partington plans
